channel.title: Midnight Archive
channel.description: The English edition of Midnight Archive: public-domain Chinese strange tales, source notes, literary retellings, and editorial reading guides.

item.title: Xi Fangping: The Man Who Would Not Withdraw His Complaint
item.description: A ghost story sharpened into a case file: justice does not arrive simply because the dead have entered another court.

item.title: Nie Xiaoqian: The Woman Brought Back from the Ruined Temple
item.description: A ruined-temple encounter becomes a story about restraint, trust, and a ghost who is not identical with the violence that trapped her.

item.title: Painted Skin: The Lovely Face That Hid a Hunger
item.description: A classic Liaozhai tale about beauty, warning signs, and the dangerous stories people tell themselves before they believe what they have seen.

item.title: The Taoist of Laoshan: Wanting Magic Without the Discipline
item.description: A light, sharp tale about a seeker who loves the spectacle of transcendence but not the labor that might make him worthy of it.

item.title: Wang Liulang: A Ghost at the River Who Refused a Victim
item.description: A gentle strange tale in which the expected water-ghost horror is turned toward friendship, restraint, and the refusal to pass suffering on.

item.title: Yingning: Laughter, Blossoms, and the Fox Girl Who Will Not Be Solved
item.description: A bright Liaozhai tale whose laughter is not shallow: Yingning's charm comes from the way she resists being completely explained.

item.title: Mountain Market: The City That Appears Like a Dream
item.description: A brief, airy Liaozhai piece about watching an impossible city form in the distance, sharpen, and vanish.

item.title: Lu Pan: After the Heart and Head Are Changed, Who Remains?
item.description: A grotesque and brilliant Liaozhai tale about talent, appearance, power, and whether a person improved by force is still the same person.

item.title: Song Dingbo Catches a Ghost: Fear Answered with Questions
item.description: A compact zhiguai tale in which a night traveler survives not by strength, but by making the ghost explain its own rules.

item.title: Seed Pear: How a Tree Grows from a Sleeve
item.description: A street-side wonder about a pear, a stingy seller, and the way small selfishness becomes public comedy.

item.title: Qingfeng: Recognition in the Lamplight of a Fox House
item.description: A fox-woman tale where love is not simply an encounter, but a negotiation with family, fear, and exposed identity.

item.title: Xiangyu: The Flower Spirit Who Remembers a Promise
item.description: A tender Liaozhai story in which flowers are not background but living vessels of memory, grief, and return.

item.title: The Cricket: A Tiny Insect Under an Empire of Pressure
item.description: A devastating Liaozhai satire in which a cricket becomes the weight of official demand, household fear, and a family's fate.

item.title: Luocha Haishi: When Beauty Turns Upside Down
item.description: A strange-seas satire where standards of beauty reverse, forcing a traveler to ask who defines ugliness, dignity, and success.

item.title: Examination for City God: Another Test After Death
item.description: A quietly absurd underworld tale that extends the examination life of the scholar into the world after death.

item.title: Jiangcheng: A Marriage Fable with Teeth
item.description: A difficult Liaozhai marriage tale that should not be flattened into a comic 'shrew' story; its real subject is power inside the household.

item.title: Ganjiang and Moye: Swords, Names, and the Memory of Revenge
item.description: An old sword legend about command, sacrifice, revenge, and how names survive violence by entering story.

item.title: Han Ping and His Wife: Twin Tombs and the Tree of Longing
item.description: A tragic legend in which private love, political force, death, and trees become one of the old world's ways of remembering grief.
